result.note: EVERYTHING-AT-ONCE end-to-end pipeline on ANIMATED 'classroom' (1920x1080, 8 frames, keyframe_every=1, bounces=12). Camera DOLLIED+PANNED+YAWED (cam_motion=1.0) for real screen-space motion + silhouette disocclusion. STEP1 REFERENCE = every frame FULLY at 4096 spp (adaptive OFF, denoise OFF) then a SLOW libvpx-vp9 slow/crf18 encode into a REAL video; T_ref = summed reference render wall-clock (2470.76s) + reference encode (1.67s). STEP2 OURS = keyframes rendered with the FULL anchor stack [adaptive sampling (thr=0.02, min=16) + oidn denoiser + albedo/normal prefiltered guides + light-tree many-light importance sampling, draft_spp=512] at full whole-subprocess wall-clock; non-key frames reprojected by Cycles motion vectors (backward-gather warp) + OUR disocclusion mask [OOB|MV-divergence|depth-discontinuity|fwd/bwd-consistency, dilated], disocclusions handled by hole_fill=rerender; the delivered frame sequence is then SPECULATIVELY TRANSCODED — a cheap libvpx-vp9 ultrafast/crf32 DRAFT of the whole clip + an SSIM-gated (gate=0.97) REAL re-encode of only the 0/8 failing segments at slow/crf18. T_ours = keyframe renders + calibration + reprojection numpy time + draft encode + measured re-encode of rejected segments. net_speedup = T_ref / T_ours = ONE measured wall-clock ratio on the SAME box (NOT a product of per-stage speedups). quality = END-TO-END SSIM of the FINAL DELIVERED VIDEO vs the TRUE REFERENCE VIDEO (both DECODED to pixels; GLOBAL + per-8x8-tile worst/p5) over 8 frames — warp error + denoiser bias + encode artifacts stacked, measured on the shipped deliverable, NOT any intermediate stage. Every render/encode TIME is real perf_counter wall-clock; every SSIM is real scikit-image on real decoded pixels. THE ONE MODELED STEP: the disocclusion crop re-render is NOT border-rendered per-frame in Cycles; its cost is charged as fixed_overhead_O + disocc_frac * keyframe_pixel_trace_P, where fixed_overhead_O=3.617s is the MEASURED Blender start + .blend load + BVH cost a real crop pays in FULL (NOT scaled by area) and keyframe_pixel_trace_P = keyframe_wall - fixed_overhead_O. Honest and CONSERVATIVE (a resident-Blender pipeline would amortize O across crops) — NOT an upper bound. Because that crop-trace time is DERIVED (area-scaled from a measured full trace) it is the ONLY non-directly-measured number, so modeled=false. The disocclusion PATCH pixels are the ANCHOR-QUALITY render of that frame (NOT the 4096-spp reference), so no patch scores SSIM~1 by construction and the delivered-frame quality stays a faithful measurement. reencode_measured=False (no rejects to re-encode).
